name: Coll d'Arca and Montferri Sanctuary Loop
name_original: Masllorenç - Montferri - Bràfim - La Joncosa del Montmell - La Bisbal del Penedès – ruta circular
description: Cycle 32.0 miles (51.5 km) through Catalonia's Penedès region, enjoying vineyards, villages, and Montserrat views on this moderate route.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Rodonyà traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features a network of rural roads connecting small villages and historical sites, with moderate elevation changes. Cyclists can expect routes that wind through vineyards and olive groves, occasionally ascending to viewpoints or passing by local sanctuaries. The area provides a blend of open stretches and more undulating sections, suitable for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Rodonyà The most popular road cycling route is Coll d'Arca and Montferri Sanctuary Loop, a 32.0 miles (51.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes significant climbs and passes by the Montferri Sanctuary. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Bar Yoli – View of Tarragona loop from Vila-rodona, a moderate 29.9 miles (48.1 km) path. This route offers scenic views towards Tarragona and traverses rural roads. Local road cyclists also love the Straight stretch towards Masllorenç – Mazinger Z Statue loop from la Joncosa del Montmell, a 39.2 miles (63.0 km) trail leading through agricultural areas and past a unique landmark, often completed in about 2 hours 53 minutes. Road cycling around Rodonyà is defined by rolling hills, rural roads, and cultural landmarks. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to more challenging routes. The routes in Rodonyà are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Rodonyà's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Rodonyà?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes around Rodonyà, offering a diverse range of options for cyclists of all levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Rodonyà?
A: Yes, Rodonyà offers 8 easy road cycling routes that are generally suitable for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature gentler terrain and shorter distances.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Rodonyà?
A: The road cycling routes around Rodonyà are characterized by rolling hills and agricultural plains. You'll find a mix of open stretches and undulating sections, winding through vineyards and olive groves, with moderate elevation changes.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 16 difficult road cycling routes around Rodonyà. These routes often include more significant climbs and longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e972251335/coll-d-arca-and-montferri-sanctuary-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Arca and Montferri Sanctuary Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial elevation gain.

Q: What are some scenic highlights or landmarks I can see along the routes?
A: Many routes offer scenic views and pass by notable landmarks. You might encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/939679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de les Ventoses&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1107442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll d'Arca&lt;/a&gt;. Cultural sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/193275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Monastery of Santes Creus&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/514236" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montserrat Sanctuary in Montferri&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible, providing interesting stops during your ride.

Q: Are there any routes with good viewpoints?
A: Yes, several routes offer excellent viewpoints. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19903094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar Yoli – View of Tarragona loop from Vila-rodona&lt;/a&gt; provides scenic vistas towards Tarragona. You can also find natural viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6193945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Mediterranean Sea and Garraf Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Rodonyà?
A: The road cycling routes in Rodonyà are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 15 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the quiet rural roads, and the blend of natural beauty with cultural landmarks.

Q: Are most road cycling routes around Rodonyà circular?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the Rodonyà area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33872427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Straight stretch towards Masllorenç – Mazinger Z Statue loop from la Joncosa del Montmell&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bràfim – Monasterio de Santa Cruz loop from Bràfim&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Rodonyà?
A: Rodonyà generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful landscapes, ideal for longer rides. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winter cycling is also possible, though cooler temperatures and occasional rain should be expected.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Rodonyà?
A: While specific parking areas are not always designated for each route, many routes start from or pass through small villages like Rodonyà or Vila-rodona, where you can typically find street parking. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking suggestions.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or cafes along the routes?
A: Many routes connect small villages, offering opportunities to stop for refreshments. While not every route will have a cafe directly on the path, you can often find local bars or small shops in the towns you pass through, such as those mentioned in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19903094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar Yoli – View of Tarragona loop from Vila-rodona&lt;/a&gt; route.
tips: It is a magnificent Modernist-style building located in the municipality of Montferri, in the Alt Camp region. Its architect, Josep Maria Jujol, took the Montserrat mountains as inspiration for his great work.
Neo-Romanesque style hermitage located on the outskirts of the town of Bràfim, in the Alt Camp. The building, built during the 19th century, stands on the top of the Puigrodó hill.

This port has two slopes, Juncosa del Montmell and Aiguaviva. Both are hard, with percentages of more than 10%, but the one that comes from Juncosa is the hardest thanks to its hard final section of 17%, with two horseshoe curves in a row that do not allow any rest. It allows to connect both the Bisbal del Penedès and Rodonyà with Aiguaviva and the road from Sant Jaume dels Domenys to the Plana de Manlleu.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for racebikes and typically takes around 2 hours and 40 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate difficulty. While it features manageable climbs and well-maintained roads, the Penedès region is known for frequent undulations and short, sharp ascents, which can be challenging. Cyclists with a reasonable level of fitness will find it achievable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route, you can start from any of the towns along the way, such as Masllorenç or La Bisbal del Penedès. Parking is generally available in these villages, though specific designated cycling parking areas are not detailed. Look for public parking options within the towns.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: You will cycle through typical mid-mountain terrain, characterized by rolling hills and manageable climbs. The roads are generally quiet and well-maintained, consisting of secondary roads and asphalted paths that wind through vineyards, wooded areas, and charming villages. Expect frequent undulations rather than long, flat stretches.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Thanks to the mild Mediterranean climate, you can cycle this route year-round. However, spring (when the vines are in full bloom) and autumn are particularly recommended for their pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, including extensive vineyards, olive groves, and charming villages. Notable landmarks include the Montserrat Sanctuary, Montferri, and the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Loreto. You'll also pass through the Coll d'Arca, offering scenic views. Distant views of the Montserrat mountains often form a stunning backdrop.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or walking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camí Ral de Vilafranca a Montblanc, the Camí de Sant Jaume de Tarragona a Lleida - Etapa 2, and the Penedès 360 Mountain Bike (ruta 5 dies), among others.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on asphalted roads. While dogs are generally welcome in the region, bringing a dog on a road cycling route typically requires a specialized bike trailer for their safety and comfort. The route itself does not have specific restrictions against dogs, but consider the nature of road cycling when planning.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this public road cycling route. You are free to enjoy the Penedès landscape at your leisure.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food, drinks, or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several charming villages like Masllorenç, Montferri, Bràfim, La Joncosa del Montmell, and La Bisbal del Penedès. These towns offer opportunities to find cafes, restaurants, and local accommodation, allowing you to experience the local gastronomy and culture.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a day of road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and potential for undulations, we recommend bringing appropriate cycling gear, including a helmet, water bottles, snacks, and basic bike repair tools. Sunscreen and layers of clothing are also advisable due to the Mediterranean climate and varying elevations. Don't forget your camera for the scenic views!

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of the Virgin of Loreto
